operation: First, initialize a workplane aligned to the XY plane, sketch a 30 x 20 rectangle centered at the origin, and extrude this rectangle 10 units along the positive Z axis to form the base rectangular prism. Next, select the topmost (positive Z) face of the base, create a workplane coplanar to this face, sketch an ellipse with 12 unit major radius and 8 unit minor radius centered at the workplane origin, then extrude the ellipse 5 units upward to form an elliptical boss. Finally, select the top face of the elliptical boss, create a workplane on this face, and cut a 6 mm diameter (3 mm radius), 8 mm deep cylindrical hole centered at the origin, extending downward through the entire boss and 3 mm into the underlying base.
geometry: The model is formed of three concentric vertically aligned features. At the bottom is a rectangular prism base, with a smaller elliptical cylindrical boss centered on its top surface. A straight cylindrical hole runs vertically through the center of the elliptical boss and extends partially into the rectangular base below, with all feature faces perpendicular to the flat top and bottom surfaces of the base.
function: This is a low-profile assembly component designed to act as a spacer, mounting base, or locating fixture. Its rectangular base prevents rotation and provides stable contact, the raised elliptical boss acts as an alignment feature for mating parts, and the central hole accepts standard fastening hardware to secure the component in place.
